query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Hexangular', 'Having six angles or corners', 'The passage presents a hexangular portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hexapod', 'Having six feet, six-footed', 'The reviewer described the tone as hexapod, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hiatus', 'A gap in a series, making it incomplete', 'Scholars noted a striking hiatus between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hibernal', 'Of or pertaining to winter, brumal or hiemal', 'Over time, the community learned to hibernal its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hibernian', 'Characteristic of ireland or its inhabitants, irish', 'Her hibernian became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hidebound', 'Bound with the hide of an animal', 'Scholars noted a striking hidebound between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hideous', 'Frightful, shocking, extremely ugly', 'The passage presents a hideous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hierarchy', 'A system in which members are ranked according to status', 'Scholars noted a striking hierarchy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hieroglyphics', 'A pictographic script, e.g. that of the ancient egyptians', 'The essay highlights the hieroglyphics of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hillock', 'A small hill', 'The essay highlights the hillock of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hinder', 'To make difficult to accomplish, act as obstacle', 'Critics argued that the plan might hinder the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hindmost', 'Furthest behind or nearest the rear, last', 'The essay highlights the hindmost of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hindrance', 'Something that holds back something else', 'Scholars noted a striking hindrance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hindsight', 'Recognition of the realities of a situation after it happens', 'Scholars noted a striking hindsight between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hirsute', 'Hairy, shaggy', 'Her hirsute became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Histrionic', 'Of, or relating to actors or acting', 'Over time, the community learned to histrionic its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hoard', 'To amass, usually for one''s personal collection', 'The author hoped the new policy would hoard tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hoarse', 'Afflicted by a dry, quite harsh voice', 'Scholars noted a striking hoarse between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hoary', 'White or gray with age', 'Her hoary became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hoc', 'The latin word for "this"', 'Her hoc became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Holocaust', 'A great or complete devastation or destruction', 'Her holocaust became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Homage', 'In feudalism, the oath of a vassal to honor a lord''s rights', 'Over time, the community learned to homage its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Homeopathy', 'Alternative medicine doctrine of "like cures like"', 'The essay highlights the homeopathy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Homily', 'A sermon, especially concerning a practical matter', 'He homily overlooked details that later proved decisive.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Homogeneity', 'The state or quality of being homogeneous', 'The essay highlights the homogeneity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Homogeneous', 'Consisting of parts that are all of the same kind', '“The town had a relatively homogeneous population with shared cultural traditions.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Homologous', 'Showing a degree of correspondence or similarity', 'The reviewer described the tone as homologous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Homonym', 'Word that sounds like another but has a different meaning', 'Her homonym became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Homophone', 'Word pronounced the same as another but differs in spelling', 'Scholars noted a striking homophone between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hone', 'To sharpen on a hone', 'Her hone became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Honorarium', 'Compensation for services without a predetermined value', 'Scholars noted a striking honorarium between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hoodwink', 'To deceive or trick', 'Critics argued that the plan might hoodwink the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Horde', 'A wandering troop or gang', 'Scholars noted a striking horde between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hosiery', 'Stockings or socks of any kind', 'Her hosiery became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hospitable', 'Cordial and generous towards guests', 'The passage presents a hospitable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hostility', 'The state of being hostile', 'Scholars noted a striking hostility between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hubri', 'Excessive pride or self-confidence', '“The general’s hubris led to a disastrous defeat.”', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hubris', 'Excessive pride or self-confidence', 'Her hubris became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Huckster', 'To haggle, to wrangle, or to bargain', 'Over time, the community learned to huckster its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hue', 'A gradation or variety of a color, tint', 'Scholars noted a striking hue between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Humane', 'Having regard for the health and well-being of another', 'The passage presents a humane portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Humanitarian', 'Having concern for or helping to improve people''s welfare', 'Critics argued that the plan might humanitarian the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Humanize', 'To give, cause to have the fundamental properties of a human', 'The author hoped the new policy would humanize tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Humble', 'Not proud or arrogant, modest', 'What seemed humble at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Humbug', 'To play a trick on', 'The author hoped the new policy would humbug tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Humdrum', 'Lacking variety, boring, dull', 'Scholars noted a striking humdrum between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Humiliate', 'To injure a person''s dignity and self-respect', 'Critics argued that the plan might humiliate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hummock', 'An elevated tract of land above the level of a marshy region', 'Her hummock became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Humus', 'The dark organic material in soils', 'The essay highlights the humus of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Husbandry', 'The production of edible crops or of animals for food', 'Her husbandry became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
